title: Add WITH_PROMETHEUS flag for kind Quick Start
description:
# GitHub Issue #302: Add opt-in WITH_PROMETHEUS flag to install kube-prometheus-stack with the pre-provisioned Keystone Operator dashboard in the kind Quick Start

**Category**: enhancement | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: CC-0089 (#270) shipped a full Prometheus instrumentation surface — five collectors registered on the controller-runtime registry, a `monitoring.coreos.com/v1` ServiceMonitor template gated by `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled` (`operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/servicemonitor.yaml`), and a reference Grafana dashboard at `operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json` covering reconcile p95 / error rate / rotation age / `db_sync` outcomes. Today none of that is reachable from a default kind Quick Start: `make deploy-infra` installs only `prometheus-operator-crds` (`deploy/flux-system/releases/prometheus-operator-crds.yaml`), there is no Prometheus scraping the operator's `:8080` endpoint, and the dashboard JSON sits unused on disk. The CC-0089 enablement how-to (`docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md`) tells the operator to "install Prometheus separately and import the dashboard into your Grafana" — fine for a real cluster, but for the kind Quick Start path that the README and the Extended Quick Start (`docs/quick-start-extended.md`) advertise as a five-minutes-to-first-`openstack-token-issue` experience, the metrics feature is invisible. Add an opt-in `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true` flag — the same shape as `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` (#284, CC-0097, `hack/deploy-infra.sh:65-69`) — that installs `kube-prometheus-stack` from the already-vendored `prometheus-community` HelmRepository (`deploy/flux-system/sources/prometheus-community.yaml`), pre-provisions the operator's Grafana dashboard via the Grafana sidecar's ConfigMap discovery, flips the keystone-operator chart's `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled` to `true`, and ships a "Step 4c — Open the Grafana UI" section in the Extended Quick Start alongside the existing kind-only demo UIs (Headlamp Step 4, Flux Web UI Step 4a, OpenBao UI Step 4b).

Concrete steps: (1) Add a new opt-in kind overlay at `deploy/kind/prometheus/kustomization.yaml` mirroring the layout of `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/`. The overlay declares the `monitoring` Namespace inline (`namespace.yaml`), references the existing `flux-system/prometheus-community` HelmRepository by `sourceRef.name`/`sourceRef.namespace` (no need to redeclare it — the source is already always-on for `prometheus-operator-crds`), and ships a single `HelmRelease/kube-prometheus-stack` in the `monitoring` namespace pinned to `>=65.0.0 <70.0.0` with `dependsOn: cert-manager` for webhook certs. (2) Bake the kind-tuning into the HelmRelease values (analogous to the chaos-mesh kind-tuning patch at `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml:55-87`): set `crds.enabled=false` (the always-on `prometheus-operator-crds` HelmRelease at `deploy/flux-system/releases/prometheus-operator-crds.yaml` remains the single source of truth for `monitoring.coreos.com/v1` CRDs — avoids the dual-owner reconcile flap), `alertmanager.enabled=false`, `nodeExporter.enabled=false`, `kubeStateMetrics.enabled=false` (a single-node kind cluster does not need cluster-wide kubelet/cadvisor/kube-state-metrics scraping for this demo), and trim Prometheus + Grafana resource requests to ≤100m CPU / ≤256Mi memory each, with `prometheus.prometheusSpec.retention=6h` so the on-disk PV stays small. (3) In the same HelmRelease, set `prometheus.prometheusSpec.serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues=false` and `serviceMonitorSelector: {}` so the keystone-operator's ServiceMonitor (which carries `app.kubernetes.io/*` labels but NOT `release: <stack-name>`) is actually picked up — this is the exact selector mismatch the CC-0089 how-to warns about at `docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md:40-49`. (4) Pre-provision the Keystone Operator dashboard: add a kustomize `configMapGenerator` in the overlay that wraps `operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json` into a ConfigMap in `monitoring` with the labels `grafana_dashboard: \"1\"` and `app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kube-prometheus-stack` — the Grafana sidecar (`grafana.sidecar.dashboards.enabled=true`, default in kube-prometheus-stack) auto-discovers labelled ConfigMaps and reloads Grafana, so no manual import is needed. The overlay must reference the JSON via a relative path that stays inside `deploy/kind/prometheus/` to keep the overlay `LoadRestrictionsRootOnly`-clean for `kubectl apply -k` (matching the chaos-mesh REQ-003 rationale at `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml:20-27`); copy or symlink-via-build-step the dashboard, or generate the ConfigMap at script time from `hack/deploy-infra.sh`. (5) Flip the operator chart's `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled` to `true` only when `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true`. Two options — pick (a): (a) one `kubectl patch helmrelease keystone-operator -n openstack --type=merge -p '{\"spec\":{\"values\":{\"monitoring\":{\"serviceMonitor\":{\"enabled\":true}}}}}'` from `hack/deploy-infra.sh` after the kind/prometheus overlay applies, gated on the flag — keeps the overlay self-contained, mirrors the chaos-mesh `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true` post-apply step at `:893-902`. Or (b) flip the chart default to `true` in `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml:38-42` and `values.schema.json:155-178` — defensible because the always-on flux-system stack already installs `prometheus-operator-crds`, so the ServiceMonitor renders cleanly even without a Prometheus running, but this is a wider chart-API change and should be its own decision. Go with (a) for this issue. (6) Add `WITH_PROMETHEUS` (default `false`) to `hack/deploy-infra.sh`, mirroring the `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` block at `:65-69`. When `true`: log it in the configuration banner at `:799`, apply `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/prometheus` after the base overlay (insert after the chaos-mesh apply at `:899-902`), append `kube-prometheus-stack` to the Phase 3 `wait_for_helmreleases` list at `:932-940` (between `chaos-mesh` and the end), and run the `kubectl patch` from step (5). When `false`: do nothing — the cluster comes up exactly as today. (7) Add Step 4c — Open the Grafana UI to `docs/quick-start-extended.md` alongside Steps 4 / 4a / 4b. Pattern matches the OpenBao UI section at `:317-349`: `kubectl port-forward svc/kube-prometheus-stack-grafana -n monitoring 3000:80`, document the default admin credentials (`admin` / `prom-operator` for the chart's stock value, or override via `grafana.adminPassword` in the overlay if a known-good kind password is preferred), open `http://localhost:3000`, navigate to **Dashboards → Keystone Operator** which is already loaded by the sidecar. Note the kind-only nature explicitly — production overlays do not install kube-prometheus-stack and operators must wire up their existing Prometheus per `docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md`. (8) Add a `::: tip Enabling Prometheus & Grafana` block in `docs/quick-start-extended.md` Step 3 (next to the existing chaos-mesh tip at `:229-248`) that documents `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true make deploy-infra` and the use cases (visualising CC-0089 metrics, seeing reconcile p95 / error rate live during local development). (9) Update the architecture snapshot in `docs/quick-start-extended.md:192-211` to add the kind-only `monitoring` namespace pods (kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus-*, kube-prometheus-stack-grafana-*, kube-prometheus-stack-operator-*) — gated on the flag, same style as the chaos-mesh / Headlamp / Envoy entries. (10) Add a verification snippet under Step 4c — port-forward `:9090` to confirm the `keystone-operator` scrape target is `Up` (the exact check from `docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md:111-130`), so a reader can debug a stuck dashboard without flipping back to the reference doc. (11) Cross-link: in `docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md` add a top-level note that on kind, `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true make deploy-infra` is the shortest path and the rest of the guide is for non-kind clusters. (12) Add an opt-in CI lane: a `tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml` suite that asserts (a) the kube-prometheus-stack HelmRelease becomes Ready, (b) `kubectl exec` into the Prometheus pod confirms a target with `job=\"keystone-operator\"` reports `health=\"up\"`, (c) the Grafana sidecar mounted the dashboard ConfigMap (assert via the Grafana `/api/search?query=Keystone+Operator` endpoint via a one-shot `curl` job). The suite runs only when `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true`, mirroring how the chaos suite is gated. (13) GitHub Actions: add a new `e2e-prometheus` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` that exports `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true` in the `Setup E2E infrastructure` step and runs the new chainsaw suite — symmetric to the existing `e2e-chaos` job at `:678-761` and the operator job at `:474`. The default `e2e-operator` job stays on `WITH_PROMETHEUS=false`. (14) Optional polish — add `make e2e-prometheus` to the Makefile mirroring the `make e2e-chaos` target at `:354-366`, with the same fail-fast guard (`kubectl get ns monitoring >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo 'kube-prometheus-stack is not installed; run \\\`WITH_PROMETHEUS=true make deploy-infra\\\` first' >&2; exit 1; }`).

**Motivation**: CC-0089 is the operator's first end-to-end observability surface — without it there is no SLI dashboard, no per-sub-reconciler latency view, and no rotation-age alerting story. Today, exercising the feature on a fresh kind cluster requires the developer to (a) read `docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md`, (b) install kube-prometheus-stack manually, (c) work around the `serviceMonitorSelector` mismatch documented at `:40-49`, (d) flip `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` in their helm values, and (e) manually upload the dashboard JSON via the Grafana **Dashboards → Import** flow (`docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md:96-108`). For the Quick Start audience that is a forty-minute side quest before they see a single panel; for code review of CC-0089-adjacent PRs (e.g., a new metric, a labelling change) it is friction every reviewer pays repeatedly. The asymmetry with the existing kind-only demo UIs — Headlamp (CC-0017), Flux Web UI (CC-0086 / #257), OpenBao UI (CC-0082 / #246) — is explicit: those features got a "Step 4*" section in the Quick Start the moment they shipped, and Quick Start readers actually click through them. Prometheus + Grafana for CC-0089 deserves the same treatment so the metrics catalogue is more than a `# HELP` line at `:8080/metrics`. Cost: the opt-in path adds ~3 image pulls (Prometheus, Grafana, prometheus-operator manager) on first run for users who set the flag, and one extra HelmRelease wait (~30–60s on a warm-cache laptop, longer in cold CI). With CRD ownership ceded to the always-on `prometheus-operator-crds` chart and Alertmanager / node-exporter / kube-state-metrics turned off, the HelmRelease's footprint is comparable to the chaos-mesh opt-in path. The `WITH_FLUX_CLI` (`hack/install-test-deps.sh:289-294`) and `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` (CC-0097, `hack/deploy-infra.sh:65-69`) prior art makes the deploy-script change a copy-paste-and-adjust exercise rather than a structural rework. Net: a small overlay + a shell flag + a documentation step turn an invisible feature into a clickable demo, without slowing the default Quick Start by a single second.

**Affected Areas**:
- `deploy/kind/prometheus/kustomization.yaml` (new — declares the `monitoring` Namespace, the `kube-prometheus-stack` HelmRelease, and the dashboard ConfigMap; references the existing `flux-system/prometheus-community` HelmRepository by name)
- `deploy/kind/prometheus/namespace.yaml` (new — the `monitoring` Namespace, kept inline so the overlay is self-contained per the chaos-mesh REQ-003 rationale at `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml:20-27`)
- `deploy/kind/prometheus/release.yaml` (new — `HelmRelease/kube-prometheus-stack` with kind-tuned values: `crds.enabled=false`, `alertmanager.enabled=false`, `nodeExporter.enabled=false`, `kubeStateMetrics.enabled=false`, `serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues=false`, `serviceMonitorSelector: {}`, trimmed resource requests, `prometheus.prometheusSpec.retention=6h`)
- `deploy/kind/prometheus/dashboard.configmap.yaml` (new — generated via `configMapGenerator` from `operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json`, labelled `grafana_dashboard: \"1\"` for sidecar discovery)
- `hack/deploy-infra.sh:65-69` (add `WITH_PROMETHEUS=\"${WITH_PROMETHEUS:-false}\"` next to `WITH_CHAOS_MESH`)
- `hack/deploy-infra.sh:799` (extend the configuration banner with `Prometheus stack    : ${WITH_PROMETHEUS} (set WITH_PROMETHEUS=true to install)`)
- `hack/deploy-infra.sh:899-902` (after the chaos-mesh `kubectl apply -k`, add a parallel `if [[ \"${WITH_PROMETHEUS}\" == \"true\" ]]; then kubectl apply -k \"${REPO_ROOT}/deploy/kind/prometheus\"; fi`)
- `hack/deploy-infra.sh:932-940` (extend the dynamic `helm_releases` list to append `kube-prometheus-stack` when `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true`, between the existing chaos-mesh append and the `wait_for_helmreleases` call)
- `hack/deploy-infra.sh` new function (after the WITH_PROMETHEUS apply + wait, run `kubectl patch helmrelease keystone-operator -n openstack --type=merge -p '{\"spec\":{\"values\":{\"monitoring\":{\"serviceMonitor\":{\"enabled\":true}}}}}'` and wait for the operator HelmRelease to settle to `Ready=True` again on the new values)
- `docs/quick-start-extended.md:192-211` (architecture snapshot — add `monitoring` namespace pods as kind-only entries gated on the flag)
- `docs/quick-start-extended.md:229-248` (add a `::: tip Enabling Prometheus & Grafana` block parallel to the chaos-mesh tip)
- `docs/quick-start-extended.md` after Step 4b at `:317-349` (new Step 4c — Open the Grafana UI section: port-forward, default admin credentials, dashboard navigation, Prometheus targets sanity check)
- `docs/quick-start-extended.md:252-253, 277` (cross-link the new Step 4c from the Step 4 paragraph that lists "Steps 4–4b" so readers find it)
- `docs/quick-start.md` (compact Quick Start — single-line tip pointing at the Extended Quick Start's Step 4c; do NOT inline the full UI tour to keep the compact path compact)
- `docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md:23-54` (top-level note: "On kind, `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true make deploy-infra` is the shortest path; the steps below cover non-kind clusters")
- `tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml` (new — asserts HelmRelease Ready, `keystone-operator` Prometheus target up, Grafana dashboard discovered)
- `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` (new `e2e-prometheus` matrix entry mirroring `e2e-chaos` at `:678-761`; the existing `e2e-operator` job at `:474` stays on the default flag)
- `.github/actions/setup-e2e-infra/action.yml` (thread `WITH_PROMETHEUS` through the env if the action wraps the variable list — same shape as `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` should already be threaded)
- `Makefile` (optional `make e2e-prometheus` target with the same fail-fast guard pattern as `make e2e-chaos` at `:354-366`)
- `architecture/docs/09-implementation/` (new short doc — or an addition to the CC-0089 implementation note — that records the kind opt-in posture: production users wire their own Prometheus, kind users opt-in via the flag; explicitly NOT added to the production `deploy/flux-system/` stack)
- `.planwerk/review_patterns/document-intentional-environment-divergence-in-overlays.md` (extend with the prometheus opt-in as another worked example alongside the existing chaos-mesh / Envoy / OpenBao UI references)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `make deploy-infra` on a fresh kind cluster (no `WITH_PROMETHEUS` set) finishes without ever creating a `monitoring` namespace, kube-prometheus-stack HelmRelease, or Prometheus/Grafana pod; `kubectl get ns monitoring` returns `NotFound`; default deploy time is identical to before (no measurable regression)
- [ ] `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true make deploy-infra` reaches the following end state: `HelmRelease/kube-prometheus-stack` `Ready=True` in `monitoring`, `Deployment/kube-prometheus-stack-grafana` `Available`, `StatefulSet/prometheus-kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus` `Ready`, the `keystone-operator` HelmRelease's rendered chart sets `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` and the resulting `ServiceMonitor/keystone-operator` is present in `openstack`
- [ ] Inside the kind cluster, the Prometheus instance reports the `keystone-operator` job as `Up`: `curl -sf http://localhost:9090/api/v1/targets?state=active | jq '.data.activeTargets[] | select(.labels.job==\"keystone-operator\") | .health'` returns `\"up\"` (asserted by the new chainsaw suite)
- [ ] Grafana auto-discovers the Keystone Operator dashboard via the sidecar — `curl -sfu admin:<password> http://localhost:3000/api/search?query=Keystone+Operator | jq 'length'` returns at least `1` (asserted by the new chainsaw suite); a human opening `http://localhost:3000` finds **Dashboards → Keystone Operator** without manual import
- [ ] The Quick Start's existing CC-0089 metric queries (`docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md:137-157`) all return non-empty data after the cluster has been Ready for ≥5 minutes — verified manually once and recorded in the PR description
- [ ] The Extended Quick Start (`docs/quick-start-extended.md`) has a Step 4c — Open the Grafana UI section that mirrors the OpenBao Step 4b pattern (port-forward command, credentials block, screenshot-or-CLI-equivalent verification, kind-only disclaimer); the Step 3 architecture snapshot lists the new `monitoring` namespace pods gated on the flag; the existing Steps 4 / 4a / 4b paragraphs cross-link to 4c
- [ ] `docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md` has a top-level note that points kind readers at the new flag and keeps the rest of the guide as the canonical non-kind path
- [ ] `kube-prometheus-stack` does NOT create or update `monitoring.coreos.com/v1` CRDs (verified by `kubectl describe crd servicemonitors.monitoring.coreos.com` showing the existing `app.kubernetes.io/managed-by` label from `prometheus-operator-crds`, NOT a flap); `crds.enabled=false` is set in the rendered HelmRelease values
- [ ] Default kind cluster bring-up wall-clock with `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true` does not exceed `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true` by more than 30s on a warm-cache laptop (target: parity ±30s; recorded in the PR description as evidence)
- [ ] `make e2e-prometheus` against a `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true` cluster passes the new chainsaw suite; against a default cluster the target fails fast with the message `kube-prometheus-stack is not installed; run \`WITH_PROMETHEUS=true make deploy-infra\` first`
- [ ] The new `e2e-prometheus` GitHub Actions job runs the chainsaw suite against a `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true` cluster; the existing `e2e-operator` job's wall-clock time on the Blacksmith runner is unchanged (no regression — it stays on the default flag)
- [ ] `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` is unchanged: the production overlay does NOT install kube-prometheus-stack — that is a customer-side decision, identical to the chaos-mesh / Envoy / Headlamp posture
- [ ] The Grafana dashboard JSON in `operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json` remains the single source of truth — the kind overlay references it via `configMapGenerator`, NOT a fork; `operators/keystone/dashboards/dashboard_test.go` continues to pass against the same file

**Non-Goals**:
- Installing Prometheus / Grafana / Alertmanager on the production `deploy/flux-system/` overlay. CC-0089 deliberately ships only the operator-side instrumentation surface; the choice of monitoring stack is a customer-side decision (kube-prometheus-stack, VictoriaMetrics-operator, Grafana Cloud Agent, …) and forge does not pick one. The kind overlay is a kind-only convenience, identical to the Envoy Gateway / Headlamp / OpenBao UI posture.
- Enabling Alertmanager, node-exporter, or kube-state-metrics. The kind opt-in is scoped to "see the Keystone Operator metrics in Grafana"; cluster-wide observability is out of scope for the demo and only adds image pulls + memory pressure on a single-node kind cluster.
- Wiring up Prometheus alerts on the CC-0089 metrics (rotation-age threshold, db_sync failure rate, reconcile p95 SLO). Alerting needs a customer-side Alertmanager target, which is exactly the decision this issue declines to make.
- Exposing Grafana via the `openstack-gw` Gateway with a `grafana.127-0-0-1.nip.io` route. The OpenBao UI (Step 4b) and the Flux Web UI (Step 4a) both ship as port-forward-only kind conveniences — Grafana follows the same pattern. A nip.io Gateway route can come later if there is demand.
- Changing the keystone-operator chart's `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled` default to `true` for everyone. That is option (b) under step (5) above and is intentionally out of scope; the kind overlay flips it via `kubectl patch` so external chart consumers see no behavioural change.
- Migrating away from `prometheus-operator-crds` (the always-on chart) to letting kube-prometheus-stack manage its own CRDs. That would create a CRD-ownership conflict the moment a user installs kube-prometheus-stack twice (e.g., once via this overlay, once on their own); ceding CRD ownership to the always-on chart keeps the model simple.
- Adding a new `kube-prometheus-stack` HelmRepository in `deploy/flux-system/sources/`. The existing `prometheus-community` source (`deploy/flux-system/sources/prometheus-community.yaml`, OCI from `ghcr.io/prometheus-community/charts`) already serves both the always-on `prometheus-operator-crds` chart and the new kube-prometheus-stack — re-use, do not duplicate.
- Auto-detecting whether the user is "about to" run a metrics demo and toggling the flag. Explicit opt-in mirrors the established `WITH_FLUX_CLI` / `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` pattern and avoids a magic coupling between the deploy script and the developer's intent.

**References**:
- CC-0089 / #270 — original Prometheus instrumentation + ServiceMonitor + dashboard PR; commit `c185c33` introduces all five collectors and the dashboard JSON consumed by this issue
- CC-0089 enablement how-to: `docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md` (the existing non-kind path that this issue shortens for kind)
- CC-0089 metric catalogue: `docs/reference/keystone-operator-metrics.md`
- Existing kind-only addon prior art: CC-0082 / #246 (OpenBao UI), CC-0086 / #257 (Flux Web UI), CC-0088 / #265 (Envoy Gateway + `openstack-gw`), CC-0097 / #284 (Chaos Mesh opt-in — the closest structural analog for this issue)
- Opt-in flag pattern: `WITH_FLUX_CLI` at `hack/install-test-deps.sh:289-294`, `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` at `hack/deploy-infra.sh:65-69` and the `kubectl apply -k` gate at `:893-902`
- Existing prometheus-community HelmRepository (always-on, OCI): `deploy/flux-system/sources/prometheus-community.yaml`
- Existing CRD ownership: `deploy/flux-system/releases/prometheus-operator-crds.yaml` (the always-on chart that owns `monitoring.coreos.com/v1` CRDs — kept as the single owner)
- Reference Grafana dashboard JSON: `operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json` and the drift-guard test at `operators/keystone/dashboards/dashboard_test.go`
- Operator chart's existing ServiceMonitor template: `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/servicemonitor.yaml`, gated by the default-`false` value at `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml:36-42`
- Chaos Mesh overlay structure that this issue mirrors: `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml`, `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/release.yaml`, `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/source.yaml`, `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/namespace.yaml`
- Architecture posture for kind-only addons: `architecture/docs/09-implementation/10-chaos-e2e-testing.md`, `.planwerk/review_patterns/document-intentional-environment-divergence-in-overlays.md`

**Labels:** enhancement

**Labels:** enhancement

stories.title: Quick Start user opts in to a clickable Grafana dashboard for CC-0089 metrics on kind
stories.role: Quick Start user (developer evaluating forge on a fresh kind cluster)
stories.want: to set WITH_PROMETHEUS=true and have kube-prometheus-stack + the pre-provisioned Keystone Operator dashboard come up automatically
stories.so_that: I can see reconcile p95 / error rate / rotation age live in Grafana within minutes, without reading the non-kind enablement guide or hand-importing JSON (CC-0100)
criteria: Running `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true make deploy-infra` on a fresh kind cluster ends with HelmRelease/kube-prometheus-stack Ready=True in the `monitoring` namespace

After bring-up, `kubectl port-forward svc/kube-prometheus-stack-grafana -n monitoring 3000:80` and opening http://localhost:3000 with admin/prom-operator shows a `Dashboards → Keystone Operator` entry without manual import
The Prometheus instance shows `keystone-operator` job target as `Up` at http://localhost:9090/targets within 90s of HelmRelease Ready
Step 4c — Open the Grafana UI in `docs/quick-start-extended.md` lists the exact port-forward command, default admin credentials, and the dashboard navigation path
Setting WITH_PROMETHEUS=false (or leaving it unset) leaves the cluster identical to today: `kubectl get ns monitoring` returns NotFound and no Prometheus/Grafana pods are scheduled

stories.title: Default Quick Start user is unaffected when WITH_PROMETHEUS is unset
stories.role: Quick Start user (developer running default `make deploy-infra`)
stories.want: the default kind Quick Start to remain unchanged in surface area and wall-clock time
stories.so_that: users who do not need Prometheus do not pay the install cost or learn a new flag (CC-0100)
criteria: `make deploy-infra` without WITH_PROMETHEUS finishes with no `monitoring` namespace, no kube-prometheus-stack HelmRelease, and no Prometheus/Grafana pods scheduled
The `keystone-operator` HelmRelease's rendered values keep `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=false` (chart default) — no ServiceMonitor exists in the `openstack` namespace
Default deploy wall-clock is unchanged within ±10s on a warm-cache laptop (recorded in PR description)
`deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` is unchanged — the production overlay does not gain any kube-prometheus-stack reference

The dashboard ConfigMap in the `monitoring` namespace references `operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json` as a single source of truth — no fork is created
`operators/keystone/dashboards/dashboard_test.go` continues to pass against the same JSON
`docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md` has a top-level note pointing kind readers at the new flag and reframes the rest as the non-kind path

requirements.description: The kind opt-in overlay SHALL declare a self-contained `deploy/kind/prometheus/` kustomization that, when applied, installs kube-prometheus-stack into a dedicated `monitoring` namespace and reuses the always-on `flux-system/prometheus-community` HelmRepository (no source duplication).
requirements.rationale: Mirrors the chaos-mesh REQ-001/REQ-002 structural posture (CC-0097): the overlay must be applicable via `kubectl apply -k` under `LoadRestrictionsRootOnly` (kubectl's embedded kustomize does not expose --load-restrictor), and the existing OCI HelmRepository already serves the chart channel, so duplicating it is a maintenance hazard.

scenarios.name: Overlay renders cleanly under LoadRestrictionsRootOnly
scenarios.when: an operator runs `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/prometheus`
scenarios.then: the apply succeeds without `--load-restrictor` and creates the `monitoring` Namespace, the kube-prometheus-stack HelmRelease, and the dashboard ConfigMap
and_then: no resource references parent directories via `../../`
the HelmRelease's sourceRef points at `name: prometheus-community, namespace: flux-system`

scenarios.name: Overlay is not part of the default base
scenarios.when: an operator runs `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/base`
scenarios.then: no `monitoring` namespace, kube-prometheus-stack HelmRelease, or dashboard ConfigMap is created
and_then: `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` is unchanged
`deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` is unchanged

scenarios.name: No new HelmRepository is added under deploy/flux-system/sources/
scenarios.when: the overlay's HelmRelease is rendered
scenarios.then: its `sourceRef` resolves to the existing `prometheus-community` HelmRepository in `flux-system`
and_then: `ls deploy/flux-system/sources/` shows no new file vs. main

requirements.description: The kube-prometheus-stack HelmRelease SHALL be kind-tuned: `crds.enabled=false`, `alertmanager.enabled=false`, `nodeExporter.enabled=false`, `kubeStateMetrics.enabled=false`, Prometheus + Grafana resource requests ≤100m CPU / ≤256Mi memory each, and `prometheus.prometheusSpec.retention=6h`.
requirements.rationale: A single-node kind cluster does not need cluster-wide kubelet/cadvisor/kube-state-metrics scraping for this demo, and ceding CRD ownership to the always-on `prometheus-operator-crds` chart avoids the dual-owner reconcile flap on `monitoring.coreos.com/v1` CRDs (Non-Goals).

scenarios.name: Rendered values disable CRDs, alertmanager, node-exporter, kube-state-metrics
scenarios.when: the HelmRelease becomes Ready
scenarios.then: `kubectl get helmrelease kube-prometheus-stack -n monitoring -o jsonpath='{.spec.values}'` shows the four flags set to false
and_then: no `kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus-node-exporter-*` DaemonSet exists
no `kube-prometheus-stack-kube-state-metrics-*` Deployment exists
no Alertmanager StatefulSet exists in `monitoring`

scenarios.name: ServiceMonitor CRDs remain owned by prometheus-operator-crds
scenarios.when: the HelmRelease has been Ready for ≥30s
scenarios.then: `kubectl describe crd servicemonitors.monitoring.coreos.com` shows `app.kubernetes.io/managed-by` from the `prometheus-operator-crds` chart, NOT a flap to/from kube-prometheus-stack
and_then: no annotation thrash is visible in `kubectl get crd … -o yaml | grep meta.helm.sh`

scenarios.name: Prometheus retention is bounded for kind
scenarios.when: the rendered HelmRelease values are inspected
scenarios.then: `prometheus.prometheusSpec.retention` equals `6h`
and_then: the Prometheus PVC is sized for the 6h budget (small)

requirements.description: The kube-prometheus-stack HelmRelease SHALL set `prometheus.prometheusSpec.serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues=false` and `prometheus.prometheusSpec.serviceMonitorSelector: {}` so the keystone-operator's ServiceMonitor (which carries `app.kubernetes.io/*` labels but NOT a `release: <stack-name>` label) is picked up automatically.
requirements.rationale: This is the exact selector mismatch the CC-0089 how-to warns about at `docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md:40-49`. Without this setting, the chart defaults to selecting only ServiceMonitors with the chart's own release label, which the operator chart does not set.

scenarios.name: Operator ServiceMonitor is scraped
scenarios.when: WITH_PROMETHEUS=true is set, the operator chart's ServiceMonitor is enabled, and Prometheus has been Ready for ≥60s
scenarios.then: `/api/v1/targets?state=active | jq '.data.activeTargets[] | select(.labels.job=="keystone-operator") | .health'` returns `"up"`
and_then: the target list shows endpoints from the operator's metrics Service in `openstack`

scenarios.name: Selector is empty (matches all)
scenarios.when: the rendered HelmRelease values are inspected
scenarios.then: `prometheus.prometheusSpec.serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues=false` and `serviceMonitorSelector` is an empty object `{}`
and_then: the same applies to `serviceMonitorNamespaceSelector` (empty = all namespaces) so the `openstack`-namespaced ServiceMonitor is reachable

scenarios.name: No release label is required on the operator chart
scenarios.when: the operator chart is installed without a `release: <stack-name>` label
scenarios.then: the ServiceMonitor is still discovered by Prometheus
and_then: no follow-up patch on the operator chart's labels is required by this change

requirements.description: The kind overlay SHALL pre-provision the Keystone Operator Grafana dashboard via a kustomize `configMapGenerator` that wraps `operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json`, with the labels `grafana_dashboard: "1"` and `app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kube-prometheus-stack`, in the `monitoring` namespace.
requirements.rationale: The Grafana sidecar (`grafana.sidecar.dashboards.enabled=true`, default in kube-prometheus-stack) auto-discovers labelled ConfigMaps and reloads Grafana — no manual import is needed. The configMapGenerator must reference the JSON via a path that stays inside `deploy/kind/prometheus/` to keep `LoadRestrictionsRootOnly` clean.

scenarios.name: Dashboard is auto-discovered by Grafana
scenarios.when: the HelmRelease has been Ready for ≥60s
scenarios.then: `curl -sfu admin:<password> http://localhost:3000/api/search?query=Keystone+Operator | jq 'length'` returns at least `1`
and_then: opening http://localhost:3000 → Dashboards lists `Keystone Operator` without manual import

scenarios.name: Dashboard JSON remains a single source of truth
scenarios.when: the overlay is applied
scenarios.then: the ConfigMap content matches `operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json` byte-for-byte (no fork, no copy-checked-in variant)
and_then: `operators/keystone/dashboards/dashboard_test.go` still passes against the unchanged file

scenarios.name: Overlay stays LoadRestrictionsRootOnly-clean
scenarios.when: the configMapGenerator references the dashboard
scenarios.then: the path is a sibling under `deploy/kind/prometheus/` (copied or symlinked at apply-time), not a `../../operators/...` parent traversal
and_then: `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/prometheus` succeeds without `--load-restrictor`

requirements.description: `hack/deploy-infra.sh` SHALL recognise `WITH_PROMETHEUS` (default `false`) as a top-level opt-in flag mirroring `WITH_CHAOS_MESH`. When `true`, it SHALL apply the kind/prometheus overlay after the chaos-mesh overlay, append `kube-prometheus-stack` to the Phase 3 `wait_for_helmreleases` list, and patch the `keystone-operator` HelmRelease values to set `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true`. When `false`, no monitoring resources are created.
requirements.rationale: Reuses the established opt-in pattern from CC-0097 so the script's structure stays uniform. The patch on `keystone-operator` is gated behind the flag to keep the chart default behaviour untouched for external chart consumers (Non-Goal: changing the chart default).

scenarios.name: Flag default is false
scenarios.when: `make deploy-infra` is run without exporting WITH_PROMETHEUS
scenarios.then: the script logs `Prometheus stack    : false (set WITH_PROMETHEUS=true to install)` in the configuration banner
and_then: no `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/prometheus` runs
no `kubectl patch helmrelease keystone-operator …` runs
the wait_for_helmreleases list does not include kube-prometheus-stack

scenarios.name: Flag = true installs the stack and patches the operator HelmRelease
scenarios.when: `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true make deploy-infra` is run
scenarios.then: the script applies `deploy/kind/prometheus`, waits for `kube-prometheus-stack` to become Ready, then runs `kubectl patch helmrelease keystone-operator -n openstack --type=merge -p '{"spec":{"values":{"monitoring":{"serviceMonitor":{"enabled":true}}}}}'` and waits for the operator HelmRelease to settle on the new values
and_then: ServiceMonitor `keystone-operator` exists in `openstack`
the script exits 0 on success

scenarios.name: Flag = true is logged in the configuration banner
scenarios.when: `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true make deploy-infra` is run
scenarios.then: the script's banner includes `Prometheus stack    : true (set WITH_PROMETHEUS=true to install)`
and_then: the order of banner lines is unchanged for default invocations

requirements.description: The opt-in path SHALL leave `deploy/flux-system/`, `deploy/kind/base/`, and `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml` (chart default) byte-identical to main: no production overlay change, no chart-default flip, and no new HelmRepository in `deploy/flux-system/sources/`.
requirements.rationale: This issue is explicitly scoped to a kind-only opt-in. Changing the chart default `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled` to `true` (option (b) in the description's step 5) is a wider chart-API change and is intentionally a Non-Goal. Production users continue to wire up their own Prometheus per the existing how-to.

scenarios.name: Production overlay is unchanged
scenarios.when: the diff between main and this branch is rendered
scenarios.then: `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` and every file under `deploy/flux-system/{sources,releases,namespaces.yaml,fluxinstance.yaml}` are byte-identical
and_then: no new file is added under `deploy/flux-system/sources/` or `deploy/flux-system/releases/`

scenarios.name: Operator chart default is unchanged
scenarios.when: `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml` is read
scenarios.then: `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled` is still `false` (the chart default)
and_then: `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.schema.json` has the same default for the same field

scenarios.name: Default deploy time is not regressed
scenarios.when: `make deploy-infra` runs on a fresh kind cluster without WITH_PROMETHEUS
scenarios.then: wall-clock is within ±10s of the pre-change baseline (recorded in PR description)
and_then: no extra image pulls occur (Prometheus, Grafana, prometheus-operator manager images are not pulled)

requirements.description: The Extended Quick Start (`docs/quick-start-extended.md`) SHALL gain (a) a Step 4c — Open the Grafana UI section that mirrors the OpenBao Step 4b pattern, (b) a `::: tip Enabling Prometheus & Grafana` block in Step 3 next to the chaos-mesh tip, (c) flag-gated `monitoring` namespace entries in the Step 3 `::: details What gets deployed` snapshot, and (d) cross-links from Step 4 / 4a / 4b paragraphs to Step 4c.

requirements.description: `docs/guides/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md` SHALL gain a top-level note that on kind, `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true make deploy-infra` is the shortest path, and the rest of the guide is preserved as the canonical non-kind walkthrough.
requirements.rationale: The existing how-to assumes a Prometheus is already running and walks through ServiceMonitor enablement + dashboard import. For kind users, that flow is now a 1-flag shortcut; for non-kind users (the production audience of this guide), the existing steps remain canonical.

and_then: no doc page declares two contradictory canonical paths

requirements.description: A new chainsaw suite at `tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/chainsaw-test.yaml` SHALL assert (a) HelmRelease/kube-prometheus-stack Ready=True, (b) the Prometheus instance reports the `keystone-operator` job target as `health=up`, and (c) the Grafana sidecar has loaded the Keystone Operator dashboard. The suite SHALL be gated on cluster prerequisites (presence of `monitoring` namespace) so it skips cleanly when WITH_PROMETHEUS=false.
requirements.rationale: Mirrors the chaos-mesh-health pattern (`tests/e2e/infrastructure/chaos-mesh-health`) for the kind-only opt-in. Closes the existing TODO in `tests/e2e/keystone/metrics/chainsaw-test.yaml:24-39` which deferred Prometheus target-Up polling because no Prometheus instance was present in the e2e cluster.

scenarios.name: Suite asserts HelmRelease, target health, and dashboard discovery
scenarios.when: the suite runs against a `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true` cluster
scenarios.then: all three assertions pass within 5m
and_then: the dashboard assertion uses `curl -sfu admin:<password> http://kube-prometheus-stack-grafana.monitoring.svc/api/search?query=Keystone+Operator | jq 'length>=1'` from a one-shot in-cluster pod or via `kubectl exec`

scenarios.name: Suite skips cleanly when monitoring namespace is absent
scenarios.when: the suite runs against a default cluster (no WITH_PROMETHEUS)
scenarios.then: the suite exits with SKIP (not FAIL) — same pattern as `tests/e2e/infrastructure/chaos-mesh-health`
and_then: no false positives are introduced into the default `make e2e` lane

scenarios.name: Prometheus target-Up check is honest
scenarios.when: the assertion runs
scenarios.then: it queries `http://kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus.monitoring.svc:9090/api/v1/targets?state=active` and checks `.data.activeTargets[] | select(.labels.job=="keystone-operator") | .health == "up"`
and_then: the assertion includes a sensible retry budget (e.g., poll every 10s up to 2m) to allow Prometheus to scrape at least once

requirements.description: `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` SHALL gain an `e2e-prometheus` job mirroring the `e2e-chaos` shape: it builds the operator image, sets `WITH_PROMETHEUS: "true"` in the env block of the `Setup E2E infrastructure` step, and runs the new chainsaw suite. The default `e2e-operator` job SHALL remain unchanged.
requirements.rationale: Symmetric to the existing `e2e-chaos` pattern at `:678-761`. Path filters (`changes` job) SHALL include the new overlay, the chainsaw suite, and the touched paths in `hack/deploy-infra.sh`, the operator chart, and the dashboard JSON so the lane runs whenever a relevant input changes.

scenarios.name: New job runs only when relevant paths change
scenarios.when: a PR touches `deploy/kind/prometheus/**`, `tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/**`, `hack/deploy-infra.sh`, the operator chart's ServiceMonitor template, or `operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json`
scenarios.then: the `changes` job emits `e2e-prometheus=true` and the lane runs
and_then: PRs that don't touch any of the above skip the lane

scenarios.name: WITH_PROMETHEUS is threaded through setup-e2e-infra
scenarios.when: the new lane runs
scenarios.then: `Setup E2E infrastructure` step sees `WITH_PROMETHEUS=true` and `hack/deploy-infra.sh` applies `deploy/kind/prometheus`
and_then: `.github/actions/setup-e2e-infra/action.yaml` threads WITH_PROMETHEUS through its `env:` block the same way it threads WITH_CHAOS_MESH today

scenarios.name: Default e2e-operator lane is unchanged
scenarios.when: the default `e2e-operator` matrix job runs against the keystone operator
scenarios.then: WITH_PROMETHEUS is unset and the cluster comes up without a `monitoring` namespace
and_then: the default lane's wall-clock on the Blacksmith runner is within ±10s of the pre-change baseline

requirements.description: A `make e2e-prometheus` target SHALL be added to `Makefile` mirroring `make e2e-chaos` (`:354-366`): it SHALL run kubectl/cluster-reachability and `monitoring`-namespace-presence preflights, then `chainsaw test` against `tests/e2e/keystone/prometheus-stack/`. It SHALL fail fast with a documented remediation hint when the namespace is missing.
requirements.rationale: Maintains CI-to-Makefile parity (per `maintain-ci-to-makefile-parity-for-new-jobs.md` review pattern) and gives developers a fast local entry point. The two preflights stay separate (per `distinguish-collapsed-failure-modes-in-preflight-checks.md`) so the kubectl-not-configured failure is not conflated with the namespace-missing failure.

scenarios.name: Target fails fast when namespace is absent
scenarios.when: `make e2e-prometheus` is run against a cluster without `monitoring`
scenarios.then: the target exits with a non-zero status and prints `kube-prometheus-stack is not installed; run \`WITH_PROMETHEUS=true make deploy-infra\` first`
and_then: no chainsaw run is started

scenarios.name: Target succeeds against a WITH_PROMETHEUS=true cluster
scenarios.when: `make e2e-prometheus` is run against a cluster where the new overlay was applied
scenarios.then: the chainsaw suite runs and exits 0 on success
and_then: the same suite is what `e2e-prometheus` GHA invokes — no parity drift

scenarios.name: kubectl reachability failure is distinct from namespace-missing failure
scenarios.when: `make e2e-prometheus` is run with no kubeconfig pointing at a reachable cluster
scenarios.then: the first preflight prints `kubectl is not configured or no cluster is reachable` and exits non-zero
and_then: the second preflight (namespace check) does not run, so its message does not appear

requirements.description: Architecture documentation SHALL record the kind-only posture: production overlays do not install kube-prometheus-stack, kind users opt in via WITH_PROMETHEUS=true. The existing review pattern `document-intentional-environment-divergence-in-overlays.md` SHALL be extended with this case as a worked example.
requirements.rationale: Keeps the architecture record honest about which addons are kind-only opt-ins (Envoy Gateway, Headlamp, Flux Web UI, OpenBao UI, Chaos Mesh — and now Prometheus). Future contributors must not assume the overlay is part of production.

and_then: the doc cites `WITH_PROMETHEUS` by name and links to the Step 4c section

scenarios.name: Review pattern is extended with the prometheus example

scenarios.then: the file lists the prometheus opt-in alongside the existing chaos-mesh / Envoy / OpenBao UI worked examples
and_then: the `Occurrences` count is incremented to reflect the new occurrence

tasks.title: Create `deploy/kind/prometheus/namespace.yaml` declaring the `monitoring` Namespace inline (CC-0100, REQ-001). Mirror the chaos-mesh `namespace.yaml` style — SPDX header + minimal metadata, no PodSecurity label needed for Prometheus/Grafana.

Create `deploy/kind/prometheus/release.yaml` with `HelmRelease/kube-prometheus-stack` in `monitoring`, version `>=65.0.0 <70.0.0`, `dependsOn: cert-manager`, sourceRef pointing at `prometheus-community` in `flux-system`. Bake kind-tuned values: `crds.enabled=false`, `alertmanager.enabled=false`, `nodeExporter.enabled=false`, `kubeStateMetrics.enabled=false`, `prometheus.prometheusSpec.retention=6h`, `serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues=false`, `serviceMonitorSelector: {}`, `serviceMonitorNamespaceSelector: {}`, Prometheus + Grafana resources ≤100m CPU / ≤256Mi mem (CC-0100, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003).

Create `deploy/kind/prometheus/kustomization.yaml` mirroring `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml`: list `namespace.yaml` + `release.yaml` under `resources`, set `namespace: monitoring`, and add a `configMapGenerator` for the dashboard with labels `grafana_dashboard: "1"` and `app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kube-prometheus-stack`. Add a top-of-file comment documenting the `LoadRestrictionsRootOnly` constraint and the dashboard-as-single-source-of-truth rationale (CC-0100, REQ-001, REQ-004).

Wire the dashboard JSON into the overlay without a parent-dir traversal: add a step to `hack/deploy-infra.sh` (gated on WITH_PROMETHEUS=true) that copies `operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json` into `deploy/kind/prometheus/keystone-operator.json` immediately before `kubectl apply -k` runs, OR add a Make target / pre-apply hook that performs the same copy. Keep the copy idempotent and ensure `git status` after `make deploy-infra` shows no unexpected modifications. Document the choice with a comment referencing REQ-004 (CC-0100, REQ-004).

Add `WITH_PROMETHEUS="${WITH_PROMETHEUS:-false}"` to `hack/deploy-infra.sh` next to the `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` block (`:65-69`) with a 3-4 line comment matching the style of the chaos-mesh comment. Append `Prometheus stack    : ${WITH_PROMETHEUS} (set WITH_PROMETHEUS=true to install)` to the configuration banner at `:799` (CC-0100, REQ-005).

In `hack/deploy-infra.sh`, after the chaos-mesh `kubectl apply -k` block (`:893-902`), add a parallel `if [[ "${WITH_PROMETHEUS}" == "true" ]]; then kubectl apply -k "${REPO_ROOT}/deploy/kind/prometheus"; log "Prometheus kind overlay applied (WITH_PROMETHEUS=true)."; fi`. Place the dashboard-copy step from task 1.4 immediately before the apply (CC-0100, REQ-005).

Extend the dynamic Phase 3 `helm_releases` list in `hack/deploy-infra.sh:932-940` to append `kube-prometheus-stack` when WITH_PROMETHEUS=true, after the existing chaos-mesh append and before `wait_for_helmreleases` is invoked. Verify ordering does not break the existing chaos-mesh case (CC-0100, REQ-005).

Add a new helper function in `hack/deploy-infra.sh` (e.g. `enable_keystone_operator_servicemonitor`) that runs after the Prometheus HelmRelease becomes Ready: `kubectl patch helmrelease keystone-operator -n openstack --type=merge -p '{"spec":{"values":{"monitoring":{"serviceMonitor":{"enabled":true}}}}}'`, then waits for the keystone-operator HelmRelease to reconcile back to Ready=True on the new values. Call it from `main()` only when WITH_PROMETHEUS=true (CC-0100, REQ-005).

implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURE: kind-only opt-in overlay mirroring CC-0097 (chaos-mesh). The new overlay at `deploy/kind/prometheus/` is self-contained (no parent-dir kustomize references), reuses the always-on `flux-system/prometheus-community` HelmRepository, and ships kube-prometheus-stack with kind-tuned values that explicitly disable Alertmanager / nodeExporter / kubeStateMetrics / CRD-management. The `prometheus-operator-crds` HelmRelease at `deploy/flux-system/releases/prometheus-operator-crds.yaml` remains the single owner of `monitoring.coreos.com/v1` CRDs. The dashboard at `operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json` stays the single source of truth — the overlay references it via configMapGenerator (with a copy-on-apply step in `hack/deploy-infra.sh` to keep the path inside the overlay root, satisfying `LoadRestrictionsRootOnly`).

OPT-IN FLOW:
```
   developer shell                  hack/deploy-infra.sh                  cluster
   ───────────────                  ────────────────────                  ───────
   WITH_PROMETHEUS=true   ──>   read flag (default false)
      make deploy-infra            log banner line
                                    ...steps 1-3 unchanged...
                                    if true: kubectl apply -k
                                       deploy/kind/prometheus    ──>   monitoring ns + HR + ConfigMap
                                    Phase 3 wait_for_helmreleases:
                                       append kube-prometheus-stack ──>  HR Ready
                                    if true: kubectl patch helmrelease
                                       keystone-operator ...        ──>  ServiceMonitor created
                                    wait keystone-operator HR Ready
                                    ...steps 5-8 unchanged...
   kubectl port-forward            (no further steps)
      svc/...-grafana 3000:80                                       <──   Grafana UI on :3000
```

DATA FLOW (metric → dashboard):
```
  keystone-operator pod  ── /metrics on :8080 ──>  Service (operator)
      ▲                                                  │
      │ scrape                                           │ selected by
      │                                                  ▼
  Prometheus (monitoring) <── ServiceMonitor (openstack, app.kubernetes.io/* labels)
      │                              (rendered when monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true)
      │ remote-read
      ▼
  Grafana ── dashboard JSON via sidecar ──  ConfigMap (monitoring, grafana_dashboard=1)
                                                  ▲
                                                  │ generated from operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json
                                                  │ by deploy/kind/prometheus/kustomization.yaml
```

DATA-FLOW MITIGATIONS: serviceMonitorSelector: {} + serviceMonitorNamespaceSelector: {} (REQ-003) ensures the operator's `app.kubernetes.io/*`-labelled ServiceMonitor in `openstack` is picked up by a Prometheus running in `monitoring` (cross-namespace) — this is the exact selector mismatch the CC-0089 how-to warns about. crds.enabled=false (REQ-002) avoids the CRD-ownership flap with the always-on `prometheus-operator-crds` chart.

PITFALLS:
- Do NOT add a new HelmRepository under `deploy/flux-system/sources/` — the existing `prometheus-community` source already serves both `prometheus-operator-crds` and kube-prometheus-stack.
- Do NOT change `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml` default for `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled` — that is a chart-API change and is a Non-Goal.
- Do NOT fork the dashboard JSON into the overlay — keep it referenced via configMapGenerator with a copy-on-apply step gated on WITH_PROMETHEUS.
- The `keystone-operator` HelmRelease in `deploy/flux-system/releases/keystone-operator.yaml` is suspended in the kind base overlay (`deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml`); the patch in task 2.4 must verify which release is the live target (helm-installed via `hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh` in CI) and patch the right one — see RISK-7.
- Keep the overlay LoadRestrictionsRootOnly-clean: no `../../operators/...` paths in kustomization.yaml.

REVIEW PATTERNS APPLIED: distinguish-collapsed-failure-modes-in-preflight-checks (Make target preflights), maintain-ci-to-makefile-parity-for-new-jobs (e2e-prometheus in both CI and Makefile), document-intentional-environment-divergence-in-overlays (extension as a worked example), ci-workflow-path-triggers-must-cover-all-file-dependencies (REQ-010 path filter coverage).
